symptom: no symptoms; discovered incidentally on a routine chest film
hook: the heart was pointing to the right and the stomach bubble sat under the wrong lung
whatYouSee: The heart points to the right and the stomach gas bubble and organs are all flipped to the opposite side, a mirror image of normal.
whyItMatters: It is usually benign, but recognising it prevents diagnostic errors and missed lateralised disease; with primary ciliary dyskinesia it forms Kartagener syndrome.
treatment: No treatment is required for the mirror anatomy itself; associated conditions such as Kartagener syndrome are managed on their own.
takeaway: A heart pointing right with flipped organs is situs inversus, a mirror-image body.

condition.symptom: no symptoms; discovered incidentally on a routine chest film
condition.hook: the heart was pointing to the right and the stomach bubble sat under the wrong lung
condition.keyFindings: the cardiac apex and aortic arch on the right (dextrocardia) with the gastric air bubble under the right hemidiaphragm and liver shadow on the left, a complete mirror-image of normal anatomy
condition.whatYouSee: The heart points to the right and the stomach gas bubble and organs are all flipped to the opposite side, a mirror image of normal.
condition.whyItMatters: It is usually benign, but recognising it prevents diagnostic errors and missed lateralised disease; with primary ciliary dyskinesia it forms Kartagener syndrome.
condition.treatment: No treatment is required for the mirror anatomy itself; associated conditions such as Kartagener syndrome are managed on their own.
condition.takeaway: A heart pointing right with flipped organs is situs inversus, a mirror-image body.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Situs inversus totalis
👀 What you see:
The heart points to the right and the stomach gas bubble and organs are all flipped to the opposite side, a mirror image of normal.
🦴 Why it matters:
It is usually benign, but recognising it prevents diagnostic errors and missed lateralised disease; with primary ciliary dyskinesia it forms Kartagener syndrome.
💊 Treatment:
No treatment is required for the mirror anatomy itself; associated conditions such as Kartagener syndrome are managed on their own.
📝 Takeaway:
A heart pointing right with flipped organs is situs inversus, a mirror-image body.
